stories.title: The Red Ball
sentences: Tom has a red ball.
He can toss it up high.
The ball hits the wall.
Tom runs to get it back.
He loves his red ball!

stories.title: My Pet Dog
sentences: I have a dog named Max.
Max has brown and white fur.
He can sit and shake hands.
Max loves to run in the park.
He is my best friend!

stories.title: The Big Tree
sentences: There is a big tree in my yard.
It has green leaves.
Birds sit in its branches.
I love to sit under it.
The tree keeps me cool!

stories.title: Mia and the Rain
sentences: One morning, Mia looked out the window.
It was raining hard outside.
She put on her yellow raincoat and boots.
She jumped in every puddle she saw.
Rain is not so bad after all!

stories.title: The Lost Kitten
sentences: A tiny kitten sat by the road.
She cried and looked around.
Sam heard her and stopped.
He gave her milk and a warm bed.
The kitten purred and fell asleep.

stories.title: The Magic Seeds
sentences: Priya found three tiny seeds in the garden.
She planted them in soft brown soil.
Every day she watered them with love.
One morning, three colorful flowers had bloomed!
Priya danced with joy in the garden.

stories.title: The Space Adventure
sentences: Raj dreamed he was in a silver rocket.
He zoomed past the moon and the stars.
An alien waved at him from a purple planet.
Raj waved back and tossed a cookie over.
He woke up smiling — what a dream!

stories.title: The Sneaky Fox
sentences: A clever fox crept into the quiet forest.
He smelled something sweet near the beehive.
The bees buzzed and the fox stepped back.
He thought of a plan to trick them.
But the bees were smarter, and the fox ran away!

stories.title: The Old Lighthouse
sentences: The old lighthouse stood tall at the edge of the sea.
Every night, its beam swept across the dark waters.
Captain Meera climbed the spiral stairs to light the lamp.
Ships far away saw the glow and steered safely.
The lighthouse never slept — and neither did Meera.

stories.title: The River Race
sentences: The annual river race began at sunrise.
Ten boats lined up, each paddler ready.
Anand's arms ached, but he kept rowing hard.
At the final bend, he burst ahead of the rest.
He crossed the finish line and the crowd roared!
